author: Kam
content: just to give an idea of how I swing stocks; I took this trade on AVGO as well as LULU last Friday. Bought around 225, with approx $10 stop. didn't go with cons as seemed too spready. sized in small as the setup wasn't ideal and sellers came in hard last Fri. Gap-up on Monday didn't give me a chance to add with low risk unfortunately so size on this isn't what I would have liked for something that's top focus for me. Sold 1/3 yesterday at 245 (2R). 2nd target is ATH, but i otherwise I trail with a close below the 9EMA and let it run as long as holds the 21EMA on something this strong.

author: CBD Steve
content:
NVDA Earnings plan - Fake out candle has happened last 3 quarters systematically between 4:10pm-4:20pm dropping $6-$9.

November, 20th 2024 - Fake out 10 minute candle: $137.92 - $146.24 (Good sell time 40 minutes after fake out) 9:20 price is $149.62

February, 26th 2025 - Fake out 10 minute candle: $125.58 - $136.59 (Ideal sell time 40 minutes after fake out) 9:20 price is $135.42 not higher than 40 minutes after

May, 28th 2025 - Fake out 10 minute candle: $133.29 - 139.99 (Ideal sell time 40 minutes after fake out) 9:20 price is $142.73 not higher than 40 minutes after

My plan: Wait for fake out to bottom out - confirm with volume chart. Buy 100 shares of NVDA. Butt clench for 40 minutes. 4:50-4:59pm sell all shares.

Fake out will drop $7-$10 historically (3 quarters data)

Buy price - only once confirmed reversal
Risk - Extremely high (no stops)
Reward - $500-$1000 profit on 100 shares
Plan B - Hold shares for 1-3 weeks if it needs to recover 
Research - About an hr lol
Additional help - I could only go back on earnings based on 10 minute candle so there is more I could be missing
Additional Discovery - At 4am 1 day before earnings it gaps up 1-3%. 2-3 quarters. It did gap down 1 quarter.

author: Huy
content:
U broke the channel, got above $42 key level but couldn't hold. Went back for a retest into the trend line and we close outside of it. A starter position is good.

author: Huy
content: Got another one for y'all. Datadog. Its Respecting its 1w trendline. If we go down to the daily chart we can see the bullish pinbar. Its not a perfect one but a good reference for now. author: crunchybrewski
content: no higher timeframe, no thesis, and for PT I usually just ride the wave until I see weakness. Ive noticed over testing that I dont like PTs, I prefer trailing SLs bc it leads to some really good trades for me mentally. So as long as I havent been in the trade for too long for theta to screw the trade, I wait til weakness.  And I use Tradezella, its gotten much better in the past month or two.

author: Mike Y
content: Hi Tonya, I was taught this style of edge by a trader named Larry Pisavento. It’s a style that I’m now combining with the BnR and confluence with ob. Using fibs with the edge that Tony and jack is teaching is a greater confluence. But Larry Pisavento wrote a great book called “Fibonacci Ratios with pattern recognition”  He does trade reversals, a system that jack and Tony don’t condone but prefer to practice momentum and continuation.  But using it in a trending tool certainly helps a great entry on retest.
